title: Li Auto March 2026 Delivery Report and Stock Rally
text: Li Auto delivered 41,053 vehicles in March 2026, representing a 12% year-over-year increase and a substantial 55% jump from February. First-quarter deliveries totaled 95,142 units, up 2.45% annually despite a 12.9% sequential decline. The company's shares rallied 4.60% to $18.64 in premarket trading following the announcement. However, the longer-term technical picture remains challenged—the stock trades 30.46% below its 52-week high and sits 13.2% beneath its 200-day moving average, reflecting persistent bearish pressure from a September death cross. The company is strengthening its infrastructure with 517 retail locations, 552 service centers, and over 4,000 supercharging stations across China. The company also unveiled its MindVLA autonomous driving model at NVIDIA's 2026 conference and plans to launch an updated L9 model in Q2. Analyst sentiment remains cautious. The consensus rating is Hold with an average price target of $24.69. Recent downgrades from major firms have lowered targets significantly, with JP Morgan at $15.50 and Jefferies at $17.50.
